message.content:
You are fixing reviewed bugs in the Trigger.dev workers of the repo at C:\repos\oracle (all under apps/workers/). Make MINIMAL, conservative edits. Read each file and surrounding code before editing. Do NOT commit. Do NOT touch files outside apps/workers/. After editing, run `pnpm --filter @oracle/workers typecheck` (or the tsc equivalent if no such script — inspect apps/workers/package.json) and report what you ran + result.

Notes: advisory-lock fixes are CODE-ONLY (use drizzle raw `sql` with `pg_try_advisory_xact_lock(hashtext($key))` inside a `db.transaction`) — no DB migration. Preserve the two-phase batch design and the every-tick settings short-circuit gates documented in AGENTS.md. Use drizzle `sql` template; remember raw list params follow the JSON-string + jsonb_array_elements_text convention.

FILES + FIXES:

1. CONCURRENCY (highest priority) — the per-hour interjection cap is not enforced under concurrency in three workers that all write `oracleInterventions` per channel: apps/workers/src/trigger/teams-live-recall-utterance.ts, lull-interjection.ts, contradiction-watcher.ts. Each reads rate/cooldown state, then makes a multi-second model call, then inserts the intervention with NO lock or re-check. Fix pattern for each: wrap the decision-commit sequence (the part that re-checks rate/cooldown state and inserts the oracleInterventions row + posts) in a `db.transaction(async (tx) => { ... })` that FIRST acquires `pg_try_advisory_xact_lock(hashtext(<channelId>))`; if the lock is not acquired, skip posting (return a 'skipped: lock_contended' style result); after acquiring, RE-READ the rate/cooldown counts inside the tx and only insert+post if still under cap/cooldown. For lull-interjection, also claim the gap atomically: `UPDATE gaps SET status='asked' WHERE id=? AND status='open' RETURNING` before drafting, and skip if no row returned. Keep the model call OUTSIDE the transaction where possible (don't hold the lock across the LLM call if the existing structure allows re-checking after) — but the re-check + insert MUST be inside the lock. Read each file carefully; the three have different structures. Be precise and preserve existing return shapes/logging.

2. contradiction-watcher.ts — the pair-existence dedup query uses `claimAId IN [a,b] AND claimBId IN [a,b]`. Replace with the explicit unordered-pair check: `(claimAId = a AND claimBId = b) OR (claimAId = b AND claimBId = a)` (use drizzle `or(and(eq,eq), and(eq,eq))`).

3. contradiction-watcher.ts — `detectionConfidence` is passed as the threshold constant itself (tautological gate). Add an optional numeric `confidence` (1-10 or 0-1, match existing scale conventions in the file) to the contradiction adjudication Zod schema, instruct it briefly in the schema `.describe()`, thread the model's returned confidence through to the gate and into the stored `contradictions.detectionConfidence`. If the model omits it, fall back to current behavior. Keep severity handling.

4. document-ingestion.ts — evidence chunk is chosen via `chunk.text.includes(exactQuote)` which fails for quotes spanning the 150-char chunk overlap, silently falling back to chunk[0] and then failing quote validation. Fix: search the full rawText for the quote; if found, map the match offset to the chunk whose [start,end] covers it (chunks are inserted in order with known offsets — inspect how chunks are created) and attach THAT chunk as the evidence pointer; only fall back to chunk[0] if the quote truly isn't in rawText. Keep existing validation downstream.

5. document-ingestion.ts — the materialized temp file (fileBackedCachePath, created ~line 195) leaks if processing throws before the existing unlink paths. Wrap the doc-processing body so the temp file is unlinked in a `finally`. Also the per-chunk INSERT loop (~lines 251-275) is N+1 — batch into a single multi-row insert with `onConflictDoNothing` collecting returned ids, preserving order. Be careful to keep returned-id ordering aligned with chunks for the embedding/evidence mapping.

6. claim-extraction.ts — messages flipped to `'processing'` are never reset on a crash (only 'pending' is re-selected). At the TOP of the scheduled task, before selecting pending work, add a reaper: `UPDATE messages SET extraction_status='pending' WHERE extraction_status='processing' AND <updated/created timestamp> < now() - interval '2 hours'` (use the appropriate timestamp column — inspect schema usage in the file). Keep it idempotent and logged.

7. brain-synthesis.ts — version-insert + currentVersionId update + sectionClaims inserts + gap insert/resolve run as separate un-transactioned statements (~lines 550-586). Wrap them in a single `db.transaction`. Also batch the sectionClaims inserts (single multi-row `values([...])`) and resolve gaps with one `inArray` update instead of N+1 loops. And the gap-resolve update must constrain to this section + open status (add `AND sectionId=? AND status='open'`-style guard) so the model can't resolve arbitrary gaps by UUID.

8. taxonomy-reclassification.ts — each `applyProposal` handler runs multiple dependent mutations with no transaction and no change-log row on partial failure. Wrap each handler body in `db.transaction` so the move/delete/retire + the taxonomy_change_log 'applied' row commit atomically. Don't change the proposal-selection logic.

9. taxonomy-reevaluation.ts — `nameCluster` parses JSON from `runText` output via a `/\{[\s\S]*\}/` regex. Convert to a structured-output call: use the OracleAIClient `runObject` with a small Zod schema `{ name: string, purpose: string }` (mirror how other workers call runObject). Also set `cacheEligible: true` on the stable system block of the naming prompt (use makeBlock's cacheEligible). Additionally, dedupe newly-proposed clusters against EXISTING PENDING create_sub_topic proposals (not only against knowledgeSubTopics) before inserting. If runObject isn't readily available in this worker, match the import pattern used by contradiction-watcher/lull-interjection.

10. recall.ts (apps/workers/src/lib or trigger) — `sendRecallChatMessage` has no retry/error-class handling; a transient send failure re-throws and (via Trigger retry + the utterance dedup) silently drops the interjection. In teams-live-recall-utterance.ts, wrap the send so a transient send failure is logged and the task COMPLETES (does not re-throw) after the utterance message is already persisted — so it isn't retried into a dedup-skip. Be careful: only swallow the SEND error, not earlier errors.

11. trigger.config.ts — `retries.default.maxAttempts: 3` applies to per-minute cron + live tasks where re-execution is harmful. Set `maxAttempts: 1` (retries disabled) specifically on the cron/live tasks: lull-interjection, teams-live-recall-utterance, and any other scheduled cron task — set it per-task via the task's `retry` option rather than changing the global default, so batch/ingestion tasks keep retries. Inspect each task definition to add the per-task override.

If any fix needs a DB column that doesn't exist (e.g. #3 confidence is already a column per AGENTS.md — verify), do NOT create migrations; adapt to existing columns and note it. Report a concise per-file summary + typecheck result. If any item is too risky/ambiguous to change safely, leave it, add a TODO comment, and flag it clearly in your report rather than guessing.

Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.

Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:mcp-builder: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. Use when building MCP servers to integrate external APIs or services, whether in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK).
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

1	# AGENTS.md — The Oracle Developer Guide
2	
3	Read this first. It is the canonical operating guide for developers and AI coding sessions working in `C:\repos\oracle`.
4	
5	## 1. Project summary
6	
7	The Oracle is an evidence-backed enterprise knowledge graph for POP Creations / Spruce Line. Employees interact with it through chat and document uploads; workers extract operational claims with quote-level evidence; deterministic validators gate promotion into approved claims; synthesis workers maintain traceable Brain sections; admin screens review runs, caches, claims, gaps, contradictions, and taxonomy proposals. The outcome that matters is explainable business knowledge: every important answer or synthesis artifact must be traceable back to messages, document chunks, or approved claims.
8	
9	## 2. Multi-model AI note
10	
11	There is no universal ignore-file standard across AI coding tools.
12	
13	`.claudeignore` works for Claude Code.
14	
15	When using any other AI tool, paste this file as your first message and follow the instructions in the "What to ignore" section.
16	
17	## Documentation map: what to read for each task
18	
19	Always start with:
20	
21	- `AGENTS.md`
22	
23	Then load additional docs only when relevant — do not bulk-read every `.md` file.
24	
25	| Task / question | Read these docs | Usually do not need |
26	|---|---|---|
27	| Quick repo orientation | `README.md`, `AGENTS.md` | Deep docs under `docs/` unless task requires them |
28	| Modify app behavior or project-owned code | `AGENTS.md`, relevant folder-level `README.md` if present, `docs/architecture.md` if system design is affected | `docs/deployment.md` unless deploy behavior changes |
29	| Add or change AI provider adapter or model catalog | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/architecture.md` (adapter table + data flow), provider files under `packages/ai/src/providers/`, `DECISIONS.md` | Worker or webhook code |
30	| Add or change configuration, env vars, feature flags, secrets | `AGENTS.md` §11, `docs/configuration.md`, `docs/deployment.md` if prod/runtime env is affected | Unrelated architecture docs |

32	| Change deployment, Docker, CI/CD, hosting, release flow, rollback, or runtime environment | `AGENTS.md` §12, `docs/deployment.md`, `docs/configuration.md`, relevant workflow/deployment files | Local-only development docs unless needed |
33	| Change database schema, migrations, models, external IDs, or data flow | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/architecture.md`, `docs/configuration.md` if env/config is affected, `packages/db/src/schema.ts`, relevant migration/model docs | Deployment docs unless rollout/deploy behavior changes |

252	## 10. Intentional quirks and non-obvious decisions
253	
254	### One employee can have many auth identities
255	
256	Looks like:
257	`employees` still has deprecated auth columns, so `employee_identities` looks redundant.
258	
259	Actually:
260	`employee_identities` is the real auth-link table. The deprecated columns remain only as transition leftovers.
261	
262	Why:
263	One person may log in through Google and Microsoft 365 and still be the same employee.
264	
265	Do not change because:
266	Collapsing back to one auth identity per employee breaks real user linkage and RLS helpers.
267	
268	### All inference must go through `OracleAIClient`
269	
270	Looks like:
271	It would be simpler for routes or workers to call provider SDKs directly.
272	
273	Actually:
274	The provider adapters are the only supported inference boundary. They own prompt shaping, provider-native caching, reasoning translation, and usage normalization.
275	
276	Why:
277	The project depends on provider-specific caching and structured-output behavior that generic wrappers and ad hoc calls hide.
278	
279	Do not change because:
280	Direct SDK calls bypass context-pack logging, cache observability, route fallback, and consistent validation.
281	
282	### Log the actual AI result route, not only the planned route
283	
284	Looks like:
285	The caller already resolved a route before calling `OracleAIClient`, so it can log that route in `model_runs` and `model_run_usage_details`.
286	
287	Actually:
288	`ModelRouter` may dispatch to a fallback route. `OracleTextResult` and `OracleObjectResult` carry `routeId`, `provider`, `modelId`, `fellBackFromRouteId`, and `fallbackReason` after dispatch.
289	
290	Why:
291	Cost/cache dashboards and fallback debugging need the route that actually ran, while still preserving the original route when fallback happened.
292	
293	Do not change because:
294	Logging the pre-dispatch route hides provider fallback and makes cache-hit/cost accounting wrong. New AI callers should use the result metadata when writing usage rows, with the pre-resolved route only as a fallback if metadata is absent.
295	
296	### Chat retrieval is deterministic, not AI-SDK tool calling
297	
298	Looks like:
299	The chat route used to define `search_company_knowledge` and `check_open_gaps` tools, so re-adding `tools` to `providerOptions` might seem like restoring agentic retrieval.
300	
301	Actually:
302	The native provider adapters do not execute Vercel AI SDK tool definitions from `providerOptions`. The chat route now performs retrieval deterministically before the model call and injects recent messages, open gaps, approved claims, and Brain snippets into prompt blocks.
303	
304	Why:
305	All inference goes through raw provider adapters to preserve provider-native cache/usage fields. Passing AI SDK tools through this boundary looked useful but was decorative unless every native adapter learned tool orchestration.
306	
307	Do not change because:
308	Reintroducing AI SDK tools in the chat route can create a false sense that the model is searching live. Add retrieval through `searchWithRetrievalPlan()` and prompt blocks, or implement tool orchestration explicitly inside the native adapter boundary.
309	
310	### OpenRouter is enrichment-only
311	
312	Looks like:
313	`openrouter.ts` and OpenRouter-related code mean inference still runs through OpenRouter.
314	
315	Actually:
316	OpenRouter is only used to enrich the admin-side model catalog with pricing/capability metadata.
317	
318	Why:
319	Inference requires native provider features and exact usage fields; catalog enrichment does not.
320	
321	Do not change because:
322	Reintroducing OpenRouter into the inference path would erase provider-native cache and usage behavior that the rest of the system expects.
323	
324	### Explicit Vertex caches are tracked in Postgres
325	
326	Looks like:
327	The adapter could rely only on in-memory cache handles.
328	
329	Actually:
330	`provider_cached_content` is the cross-process source of truth for explicit Vertex cache lifecycle, and `provider_metadata_json` now also tracks cleanup metadata for temporary GCS-backed cache sources.
331	
332	Why:
333	Workers and web requests run in different processes and time windows. Cache accounting and cleanup have to survive process boundaries.
334	
335	Do not change because:
336	Process-local cache tracking leaks money and makes cache reuse/cleanup invisible.
337	
338	### Qwen chat cache state is persisted separately from run usage
339	
340	Looks like:
341	`provider_response_sessions` duplicates data already in `model_runs`.
342	
343	Actually:
344	`model_runs` is audit history; `provider_response_sessions` stores the latest reusable `previous_response_id` keyed by logical session.
345	
346	Why:
347	Responses API session cache needs a stable conversation handle across requests and processes.
348	
349	Do not change because:
350	Without it, Qwen session cache resets every turn and the savings disappear.
351	
352	### Batch API methods on the adapter contract are optional
353	
354	Looks like:
355	`OracleProviderAdapter.submitBatch` and `retrieveBatch` are marked `?` (optional). Existing adapters (Anthropic, DeepSeek, Qwen) don't implement them. The interface looks half-finished.
356	
357	Actually:
358	The methods are optional on purpose. DeepSeek has no public Batch API. Qwen's batch surface is non-OpenAI-compatible (DECISIONS.md D12 deferred the native DashScope SDK swap). OpenAI, Vertex, and Anthropic implement both methods today; future adapters opt in by implementing both.
359	
360	Why:
361	Forcing every adapter to implement batch would either block adding new providers behind a 50%-discount feature, or paper over it with stub `submitBatch` methods that throw — both worse than the optional pattern. The runtime helper `supportsBatch(adapter)` is the feature-detection contract. See DECISIONS.md D14.
362	
363	Do not change because:
364	Making `submitBatch` / `retrieveBatch` required on the interface would force the DeepSeek and Qwen adapters to throw at construction, which would break the per-provider `tryAdd()` boot in `buildStandardAdapters()` and silently disable both providers.
365	
366	Anthropic batch specifics: each request is a `messages.batches.create` entry with `custom_id` + `params` (same shape as a sync `messages.create`); when `jsonSchema` is provided we attach the forced single-tool input_schema per-request (mirrors `generateObject`). Status maps `processing_status: ended` → `'completed'` and we stream `messages.batches.results(id)` to produce `BatchResultItem`s. Per-item `result.type` → success (`succeeded` — tool_use input or text), or failure (`errored` / `canceled` / `expired`). `providerMetadata` is `{}` — the batch ID alone is sufficient for retrieve.
367	
368	### Two-phase batch worker — submit and drain are independent tasks
369	
370	Looks like:
371	`claim-extraction.ts`, `claim-extraction-batch-submit.ts`, and `claim-extraction-batch-drain.ts` look redundant. The flag `extraction_dispatch_mode` toggles them, but all three are scheduled.
372	
373	Actually:
374	Each task reads `settings.extraction_dispatch_mode` at the top and bails when it doesn't match its dispatch mode. So at any given time only ONE of (sync) or (submit + drain) is active. The drain task is always scheduled because it always needs to poll outstanding `provider_batch_jobs` rows even after the flag flips back to `'sync'` (any in-flight batches should still be drained, not orphaned).
375	
376	Why:
377	The flag is read every cron tick — flipping it doesn't require a redeploy. The drain task running unconditionally ensures no orphaned batches when admin flips back to sync. Per-task short-circuit at the top is the cleanest gate.
378	
379	Do not change because:
380	Removing the always-on drain task would orphan any in-flight Vertex/OpenAI batches if the flag flipped back to sync mid-stream. Removing the gate from the sync task would double-process in batch mode.
381	
382	### Model catalog quality filter runs at BOTH write and read time
383	
384	Looks like:
385	The same filter logic appears in `packages/ai/src/model-capabilities/index.ts` (`refreshModelCatalog`) AND `apps/web/app/api/admin/model-catalog/route.ts` (`passesQualityFilter`). Looks duplicated.
386	
387	Actually:
388	Intentional defense in depth. The write-time filter prevents new junk from landing in the DB. The read-time filter handles the legacy case: rows written BEFORE the write-time filter shipped (or when OpenRouter enrichment was unavailable) sit in the DB with no pricing and no capability flags. Deleting them would break pool selections that still reference deprecated model IDs. Filtering at read time hides them from admins without losing the FK target.
389	
390	Why:
391	The write-time-only filter let junk creep back into the admin UI whenever an old OpenRouter outage produced unenriched rows. The read-time filter is the catch-all.
392	
393	Do not change because:
394	Removing the read-time filter resurrects the original "junky models reappear" bug. Removing the write-time filter floods the DB with no-data rows that build up over time. Keep both.
395	
396	### Design file operations are not product/design workflow
397	
398	Looks like:
399	Design-file naming, server organization, invalid filename characters, Photoshop/Illustrator file bloat, linked assets, packaging, and archive cleanup could live under `creative_design`, `product_development`, or `it_systems` because the design team and design tools are involved.
400	
401	Actually:
402	They live in the dedicated top-level domain `design_file_operations`. That domain is for technical creative-file hygiene: keeping design files valid, lightweight, findable, compatible, packaged, versioned, and safe to share. Product/design workflow remains separate: concept intake, design assignment, proofs, approvals, revisions, production handoff, and product lifecycle state belong in `product_development`, `creative_design`, `licensing_approvals`, or `production_lifecycle`.
403	
404	Why:
405	The same employees participate in both knowledge bases, but the user intent is different. "How should I name/save/store this file?" should not retrieve product approval/status claims. "Where is this product in design approval?" should not retrieve filename/server-folder rules.
406	
407	Do not change because:
408	Collapsing these domains causes retrieval bleed between computer/file-management practices and the business workflow of designs/products moving through the company. Keep `design_file_operations` as its own retrieval target and preserve its negative boundary against product workflow domains.
409	
410	### Operations systems are not generic IT support
411	
412	Looks like:
413	ERP, CRM, PLM, Google Sheets, and integration work could all live under `it_systems`.
414	
415	Actually:
416	Business-system data-flow knowledge lives in `operations_systems`. That domain covers ERP/CRM/PLM workflows, field mapping, source-of-truth rules, validation, and integrations such as moving OrderList, MasterData, and TaskList from Google Sheets into Designflow PLM. Generic account access, password resets, permission troubleshooting, and system administration remain in `it_systems`.
417	
418	Why:
419	The Oracle needs to guide operational integration decisions, not just answer technical support questions. "How do I log into Designflow?" and "Which MasterData fields become Designflow PLM item fields?" should retrieve different evidence.
420	
421	Do not change because:
422	Collapsing `operations_systems` back into generic `it_systems` makes business-process integration knowledge compete with IT support noise and weakens retrieval for ERP/CRM/PLM data migration work.
423	
424	### Ineligible models are SELECTABLE (red checkbox), not disabled
425	
426	Looks like:
427	Models that don't meet a stage's required capabilities still have an active checkbox in the model-pool grid, just colored red.
428	
429	Actually:
430	Admin override is intentional. Sometimes a model is fine for a stage even when one of the canonical capability flags is missing (e.g. OpenRouter hasn't enriched the model yet, but the admin knows it supports tools). Disabling the checkbox would force admins to wait for enrichment or edit settings via SQL.
431	
432	Why:
433	The stage-requirements predicates are a heuristic, not ground truth. Red styling + the missing-caps hover tooltip make the override state obvious; no silent risk of accidental selection.
434	
435	Do not change because:
436	Disabling the checkbox blocks valid admin overrides and forces SQL editing.
437	
438	### Stage `thinking` requirement is on Synthesis, not Extraction
439	
440	Looks like:
441	The Extraction model card has no reasoning-effort row visible, while Synthesis does — even though extraction is the more "thinking-heavy" task by feel.
442	
443	Actually:
444	`thinking` was moved from `STAGE_REQUIREMENTS.extraction` → `STAGE_REQUIREMENTS.synthesis` on 2026-05-28 (`apps/web/lib/stage-requirements.ts`). Extraction benefits more from speed + verbatim quote fidelity; synthesis benefits from extended reasoning when consolidating a large approved-claim corpus into a single Brain section.
445	
446	Why:
447	Earlier requirement set forced extraction into reasoning models that produced slower, more elaborate JSON for no provable accuracy win. Moving `thinking` to synthesis matches actual cost/quality observations.
448	
449	Do not change because:
450	Putting `thinking` back on extraction excludes Gemini Flash and the cheap GPT-4o-mini path from the extraction pool — both proven good for extraction in our wet-tests.
451	
452	### OpenAI model catalog uses a blocklist, not an allowlist
453	
454	Looks like:
455	A new GPT or o-series model is missing from the admin catalog even though it appears in the OpenAI API response.
456	
457	Actually:
458	The source in `packages/ai/src/model-capabilities/sources/openai.ts` uses a blocklist of non-chat categories. A model whose name hits a blocked prefix or substring (`-tts`, `-transcribe`, `-search-api`, etc.) is excluded. Otherwise it passes through automatically.
459	
460	Why:
461	An allowlist required a code change for every new OpenAI model generation. GPT-5.x was invisible until `gpt-5` was manually added to the prefix list. The blocklist lets new chat models appear without a code change; only genuinely non-chat categories are excluded. See `DECISIONS.md` D13.
462	
463	Do not change because:
464	Reverting to an allowlist means future GPT-6/o-series models will be silently missing from the catalog until someone notices and edits the list. Post-enrichment quality filters (no-data models, ≥$15.01/1M input) provide a second layer of junk removal in `index.ts`.
465	
466	### Hand-written SQL migrations are authoritative for constraints, views, and data fixes
467	
468	Looks like:
469	Drizzle-generated SQL should be enough, so editing old generated files might be acceptable.
470	
471	Actually:
472	Generated files cover schema DDL only. Constraints, RLS, views, and data migrations live in `packages/db/migrations/sql/*.sql`.
473	
474	Why:
475	The migration runner applies generated DDL plus hand-written SQL in deterministic order.
476	
477	Do not change because:
478	Editing old generated files breaks replay expectations and production drift recovery.
479	
480	### Claim retrieval has exactly one path, and the two SQL branches must stay in lockstep
481	
482	Looks like:
483	`searchWithRetrievalPlan()` in `packages/ai/src/retrieval.ts` contains two near-duplicate SQL queries — a hybrid pgvector+tsvector path and a `_searchFallbackTsvector()` path — and a separate `verify:retrieval-filter-parity` script that seems redundant with typecheck.
484	
485	Actually:
486	The fallback runs only when `OPENAI_API_KEY` is unset (dev, no embeddings). Both branches build their WHERE clauses from the same private helper `buildPlanMetadataFilters()`, and the parity guard statically asserts every filter the helper returns is interpolated into BOTH branches. `searchApprovedClaims()` (a weaker, plan-less retrieval) was deleted on 2026-05-28 — there is now exactly one endorsed retrieval entry point.
487	
488	Why:
489	The recurring regression here was adding a narrowing filter to the hybrid path and forgetting the fallback, making dev-mode retrieval silently weaker (or vice versa). Typecheck can't catch it — the fragments are SQL strings. The guard runs in CI (`pr-check.yml`) and via `pnpm --filter @oracle/ai verify:retrieval-filter-parity`.
490	
491	Do not change because:
492	Adding a second retrieval path (or a filter to only one branch) reintroduces the exact silent-divergence class the guard exists to prevent. New narrowing fields go in `buildPlanMetadataFilters()` and get interpolated into both branches — nowhere else.
493	
494	### Raw-SQL list parameters are bound as JSON strings, not JS arrays
495	
496	Looks like:
497	`buildPlanMetadataFilters()` in `packages/ai/src/retrieval.ts` could use the simpler `= ANY(${arr}::text[])` form instead of `IN (SELECT jsonb_array_elements_text(${JSON.stringify(arr)}::jsonb))`.
498	
499	Actually:
500	Two driver pitfalls make the obvious forms fail at runtime (found 2026-06-10, the first day a hinted retrieval ran against an approved+embedded claim): a bare JS array in a drizzle ``sql`` template expands to a placeholder list `($1, $2)`, making `ANY((...)::text[])` a syntax error; and binding the array as one param (`sql.param`) relies on postgres-js serialization of unknown-typed params, which is unreliable. Also, a type modifier such as `vector(1536)` cannot be a bind parameter — `EMBEDDING_DIM` is inlined with `sql.raw`. The static verify guards and typecheck cannot catch any of this; it only fails when the query executes.
501	
502	Do not change because:
503	Reverting to `ANY(${arr}::text[])` reintroduces a runtime-only failure that stays invisible until real data exercises the filter. New list filters in raw SQL should follow the JSON-string + `jsonb_array_elements_text` / `jsonb_to_recordset` pattern.
504	
505	### Microsoft Graph Teams transcripts arrive after the call
506	
507	Looks like:
508	The Oracle could "sit in" a Teams call and react live, or read the live transcript panel a bot can see on screen.
509	
510	Actually:
511	Microsoft Graph exposes **no** live caption/transcript API, and Teams does not pipe spoken words into the meeting text chat. The Graph transcript path is therefore after-the-fact, and only if transcription was turned on. Ad-hoc "Meet Now" calls are reachable **only** through a `communications/adhocCalls/getAllTranscripts` change-notification subscription (beta endpoint; v1.0 rejects it), which is "listen going forward" — a transcript notifies only if the subscription existed before transcription started.
512	
513	Live spoken participation now exists through the optional Recall.ai path: Recall provides the meeting bot and STT stream; The Oracle receives finalized `transcript.data` utterances via `/api/teams/live/recall`, writes them as `messages`, and posts only gated questions back to Teams chat through Recall.
514	
515	Why:
516	Native live spoken awareness would require Microsoft media-bot infrastructure. Recall avoids changing Oracle's infrastructure by externalizing the Teams audio/STT transport while keeping Oracle's durable state in Postgres.
517	
518	Do not change because:
519	Do not confuse the two paths. Graph is post-call evidence/backfill; Recall is live optional participation. Both must still write utterances to `messages` and let candidate-before-claim handle durable knowledge. See `docs/architecture.md` § "Teams transcript ingestion" and § "Teams live participation (Recall.ai)".
520	
521	### The Graph subscription/transcript helper is duplicated on purpose
522	
523	Looks like:
524	`apps/web/lib/microsoft-graph.ts` and `apps/workers/src/lib/graph-transcripts.ts` both implement the app-only token + subscription/transcript calls. Looks like copy-paste that should be a shared package.
525	
526	Actually:
527	`apps/web` (the webhook) and `apps/workers` (the subscription manager + ingestion) are separate runtime processes, and cross-app imports aren't allowed. Both need the same small Graph surface. The web copy (`microsoft-graph.ts`) is the reference; the worker copy is intentionally self-contained.
528	
529	Do not change because:
530	Forcing a shared package for ~150 lines pulls Graph code into a third location and couples the web and worker builds. If they drift, reconcile toward the web copy rather than introducing a shared dependency.
531	
532	### ESLint config imports eslint-config-next natively, not via FlatCompat
533	
534	Looks like:
535	`apps/web/eslint.config.mjs` imports `eslint-config-next/core-web-vitals` and spreads it directly, instead of the `FlatCompat`-based pattern `create-next-app` generates.
536	
537	Actually:
538	With `eslint-config-next` v16 + ESLint 9, the `FlatCompat` path throws `TypeError: Converting circular structure to JSON` (the bundled react config has a circular `configs` object). v16 ships a native flat-config array, so importing it directly is the working path. The old `.eslintrc.json` + `next lint` were removed (Next 16 dropped the `next lint` subcommand).
539	
540	Do not change because:
541	Reverting to `FlatCompat` reintroduces the circular-structure crash; reverting to `next lint` breaks entirely (`next lint` no longer exists in Next 16).
542	
543	### Quote validation is fuzzy for transcripts, strict for documents
544	
545	Looks like:
546	`validateQuote` has an `allowFuzzy` path that accepts a quote when its tokens merely *overlap* the source — contradicting the "deterministic verbatim provenance / no fuzzy match" principle in `docs/oracle/03` and the comment at the top of `quote-validator.ts`.
547	
548	Actually:
549	Spoken Teams transcripts are disfluent and the extraction model paraphrases them, so the polished claim quote never appears verbatim in any utterance — strict matching rejected ~every transcript-derived claim. The fuzzy path (opt-in, enabled only on the message/transcript path in `claim-extraction.ts`; documents stay strict) is a **deterministic** token-overlap check (no LLM grader) and anchors the stored evidence to the **real** utterance text, not the model's paraphrase. See `DECISIONS.md` D-transcript-fuzzy-quote.
550	
551	Why:
552	Without it the entire Teams-transcript feature produces zero promotable claims. Provenance is preserved as "this real utterance supports this claim" rather than "the model copied these exact words."
553	
554	Do not change because:
555	Reverting to strict-only re-breaks transcript extraction. If tightening is wanted, raise `fuzzyMinOverlap` or restrict `allowFuzzy` to transcript-sourced messages — don't remove it.
556	
557	### `raw_transcripts` is hand-written SQL, not in schema.ts
558	
559	Looks like:
560	`raw_transcripts` (the original VTT per call) is missing from `packages/db/src/schema.ts`, and the ingestion worker reads/writes it with raw `sql` instead of Drizzle.
561	
562	Actually:
563	It's defined only in the hand-written `packages/db/migrations/sql/62_raw_transcripts.sql` (idempotent `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS`), like the observability views. Keeping it out of `schema.ts` avoids a drizzle-kit drift entry for an ancillary raw-storage table the typed query layer never needs.
564	
565	Why:
566	The VTT is stored so the whole pipeline stays re-runnable from true source after Microsoft expires the transcript (`messages` are a lossy transform). See `DECISIONS.md` D-raw-transcripts.
567	
568	Do not change because:
569	Adding it to `schema.ts` would make drizzle-kit want to generate a migration for an already-applied hand-written table (drift). Leave it as hand-written SQL.
570

1	// Contradiction watcher — spec Part 9.3 + spec 5.1 Rule 1.
2	//
3	// R11.0 refactor: all model calls now go through OracleAIClient with direct
4	// provider adapters (DECISIONS.md D6 + D9). The previous getOpenRouter() +
5	// Vercel-AI-SDK generateObject() path is retired. R3 observability rows
6	// (model_run_usage_details + oracle_context_packs) are written for every
7	// adjudication call, matching the R6 / R7 / R9 workers.
8	//
9	// Two modes:
10	//   1. Per-claim task: triggered after a new claim is approved, compares it
11	//      against existing approved claims via pgvector ANN, then calls a small
12	//      LLM to adjudicate whether a semantic match is actually contradictory.
13	//   2. Sweep cron (every 4 hours): finds recently approved claims that have
14	//      not yet been checked and triggers the per-claim task for each.
15	//
16	// Spec constraints:
17	//   - Most possible contradictions should NOT cause live interjections (spec 5.1 Rule 1).
18	//   - The setting `enable_live_contradiction_interjections` gates live interjection.
19	//   - When a contradiction is found, the default decision is 'queued_gap' or 'admin_review'.
20	//   - Log oracle_interventions row if any action is taken.
21	//   - Every LLM call → model_runs + model_run_usage_details + oracle_context_packs.
22	
23	import { schedules, task, tasks } from '@trigger.dev/sdk/v3';
24	import { and, desc, eq, gte, inArray, sql } from 'drizzle-orm';
25	import { z } from 'zod';
26	import { createHash } from 'node:crypto';
27	import { getDirectDb } from '@oracle/db/client';
28	import {
29	  claimEvidence,
30	  claimTopDomains,
31	  claims,
32	  contradictions,
33	  gaps,
34	  messages,
35	  modelRuns,
36	  modelRunUsageDetails,
37	  jobRuns,
38	  oracleContextPacks,
39	  oracleInterventions,
40	  settings,
41	} from '@oracle/db/schema';
42	import {
43	  OracleAIClient,
44	  buildStandardAdapters,
45	  embedText,
46	  getOracleRoute,
47	  resolveRouteFromSettings,
48	  makeBlock,
49	  searchWithRetrievalPlan,
50	  buildDomainScopedPlan,
51	  buildGlobalRetrievalPlan,
52	  type OracleModelRoute,
53	} from '@oracle/ai';
54	// EMBEDDING_DIM removed: ANN search now goes through searchWithRetrievalPlan
55	// which handles embedding dimensions internally.
56	import {
57	  decideContradictionInterjection,
58	  CONTRADICTION_LIVE_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD,
59	} from '@oracle/engines';
60	
61	// Extraction route is the right home for adjudication too — it's a cheap
62	// structured-output call. Different from R11's live-interjection drafting,
63	// which uses the interview route for human-facing warmth.
64	const FALLBACK_ROUTE_ID = 'vertex_gemini_2_5_flash_extraction_primary';
65	const FALLBACK_INTERVIEW_ROUTE_ID = 'anthropic_claude_haiku_4_5_interview_primary';
66	
67	// Default settings if the rows are missing — match seed defaults.
68	const DEFAULT_ORACLE_COOLDOWN_MINUTES = 10;
69	const DEFAULT_MAX_ORACLE_INTERJECTIONS_PER_HOUR = 3;
70	
71	const LIVE_INTERJECTION_PROMPT_VERSION = 'contradiction-live-1.0.0';
72	
73	const LIVE_INTERJECTION_SYSTEM = `You are the Oracle — an evidence-backed knowledge assistant for POP Creations / Spruce Line.
74	
75	Two approved claims on file appear to contradict each other. The team is mid-conversation, so surface the conflict as one warm, natural, chat-shaped question that helps resolve it. Match the tone of a colleague who quietly noticed something off.
76	
77	Hard rules:
78	- Return ONLY the question text — no preface, no explanation, no metadata.
79	- 240 characters or less.
80	- One question only.
81	- Plain text, no markdown.
82	- If the suggested question is already concrete, you can use it nearly verbatim.
83	- Do not name the model, the system, or "contradictions" — just ask which one is right today.`;
84	
85	// Similarity threshold — cosine distance below this may indicate related claims.
86	// pgvector cosine distance: 0 = identical, 2 = maximally different.
87	const SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD = 0.35;
88	
89	// How many similar claims to retrieve per comparison.
90	const TOP_K = 8;
91	
92	const CONTRADICTION_PROMPT_VERSION = 'contradiction-1.0.0';
93	
94	// ─── Module-singleton OracleAIClient (mirrors R6/R7/R8/R9 workers) ──────────
95	function buildOracleClient(): OracleAIClient {
96	  return new OracleAIClient({
97	    adapters: buildStandardAdapters(),
98	    fallbackOnError: true,
99	  });
100	}
101	
102	// ─── LLM adjudication schema: is this a contradiction? ──────────────────────
103	const ContradictionCheckSchema = z.object({
104	  isContradiction: z
105	    .boolean()
106	    .describe('True if the two claims assert conflicting facts about the same operational reality.'),
107	  explanation: z
108	    .string()
109	    .describe('One-sentence explanation of why this is or is not a contradiction.'),
110	  severity: z
111	    .enum(['low', 'medium', 'high'])
112	    .describe(
113	      'If a contradiction: how serious the conflict is. low=minor nuance, medium=significant disagreement, high=directly conflicting facts that cannot both be true.',
114	    ),
115	  suggestedQuestion: z
116	    .string()
117	    .optional()
118	    .describe(
119	      'A question that would resolve this contradiction if asked in a conversation.',
120	    ),
121	});
122	type ContradictionCheck = z.infer<typeof ContradictionCheckSchema>;
123	
124	const CONTRADICTION_ADJUDICATION_SYSTEM = `You are an operational knowledge consistency checker for The Oracle.
125	
126	Your task: determine whether two operational claims about the same company (POP Creations / Spruce Line) are contradictory.
127	
128	A CONTRADICTION exists when:
129	- Both claims address the same process, system, or operational rule, AND
130	- They assert incompatible facts (both cannot simultaneously be true).
131	
132	Not a contradiction:
133	- One claim is a general rule and the other is a recognized exception to that rule.
134	- The claims describe different aspects of the same process.
135	- The claims apply to different time periods, departments, or conditions.
136	- One claim is a refinement or elaboration of the other.
137	
138	Be precise: "We usually do X" vs "Sometimes we do Y" is NOT a contradiction.
139	"We always send to China first" vs "China receives it only after US approval" IS a contradiction.
140	
141	Return only the structured JSON matching the schema.`;
142	
143	// ─── Payload schema ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
144	const ClaimCheckPayloadSchema = z.object({
145	  claimId: z.string().uuid(),
146	});
147	
148	// ─── Curated route resolver (mirrors claim-extraction.ts) ───────────────────
149	async function resolveContradictionRoute(
150	  db: ReturnType<typeof getDirectDb>,
151	): Promise<OracleModelRoute> {
152	  const resolved = await resolveRouteFromSettings(db, 'extraction');
153	  if (resolved) return resolved;
154	  const fallback = getOracleRoute(FALLBACK_ROUTE_ID);
155	  if (!fallback) {
156	    throw new Error(
157	      `[contradiction-watcher] default_extraction_route unset/unresolvable, and fallback "${FALLBACK_ROUTE_ID}" also missing.`,
158	    );
159	  }
160	  console.warn(
161	    `[contradiction-watcher] default_extraction_route unset/unresolvable; using fallback "${FALLBACK_ROUTE_ID}".`,
162	  );
163	  return fallback;
164	}
165	
166	// ─── Single adjudication call: one (claimA, claimB) pair via OracleAIClient.
167	//     Writes all three observability rows on success or failure.
168	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
169	async function adjudicateOnePair(
170	  db: ReturnType<typeof getDirectDb>,
171	  client: OracleAIClient,
172	  route: OracleModelRoute,
173	  claimASummary: string,
174	  claimBSummary: string,
175	): Promise<{ object: ContradictionCheck | null; modelRunId: string | null; error: string | null }> {
176	  const userContent = `CLAIM A:\n${claimASummary}\n\nCLAIM B:\n${claimBSummary}`;
177	
178	  const blocks = [
179	    makeBlock({
180	      id: 'sys',
181	      label: 'Contradiction adjudication system',
182	      kind: 'stable_system',
183	      content: CONTRADICTION_ADJUDICATION_SYSTEM,
184	      cacheEligible: true,
185	      reasonIncluded: 'spec 5.1 Rule 1 — contradiction adjudication',
186	    }),
187	    makeBlock({
188	      id: 'pair',
189	      label: 'Claim pair',
190	      kind: 'dynamic_input',
191	      content: userContent,
192	      cacheEligible: false,
193	      reasonIncluded: 'current claim pair under adjudication',
194	    }),
195	  ];
196	
197	  const callStartedAt = Date.now();
198	  let modelRunId: string | null = null;
199	  try {
200	    const result = await client.runObject<ContradictionCheck>({
201	      taskType: 'contradiction_detection',
202	      routeId: route.routeId,
203	      promptVersion: CONTRADICTION_PROMPT_VERSION,
204	      blocks,
205	      schema: ContradictionCheckSchema,
206	    });
207	    const latencyMs = Date.now() - callStartedAt;
208	    const actualRouteId = result.routeId ?? route.routeId;
209	    const actualProvider = result.provider ?? route.provider;
210	    const actualModelId = result.modelId ?? route.modelId;
211	
212	    // Observability — three rows like every other R6+ worker.
213	    const [contextPack] = await db
214	      .insert(oracleContextPacks)
215	      .values({
216	        taskType: 'contradiction_detection',
217	        routeId: route.routeId,
218	        promptVersion: CONTRADICTION_PROMPT_VERSION,
219	        stablePrefixHash: hashString(CONTRADICTION_ADJUDICATION_SYSTEM),
220	        dynamicInputHash: hashString(userContent),
221	        blocksJson: blocks.map((b) => ({
222	          id: b.id,
223	          kind: b.kind,
224	          hash: b.hash,
225	          tokenEstimate: b.tokenEstimate,
226	        })),
227	      })
228	      .returning({ id: oracleContextPacks.id });
229	    if (!contextPack) throw new Error('failed to insert oracle_context_packs row');
230	
231	    const [modelRun] = await db
232	      .insert(modelRuns)
233	      .values({
234	        taskType: 'contradiction-check',
235	        model: actualModelId,
236	        provider: actualProvider,
237	        promptVersion: CONTRADICTION_PROMPT_VERSION,
238	        inputHash: hashString(CONTRADICTION_ADJUDICATION_SYSTEM),
239	        inputTokens: result.usage.inputTokens ?? null,
240	        outputTokens: result.usage.outputTokens ?? null,
241	        latencyMs,
242	        success: result.validation.ok,
243	      })
244	      .returning({ id: modelRuns.id });
245	    if (!modelRun) throw new Error('failed to insert model_runs row');
246	    modelRunId = modelRun.id;
247	
248	    await db.insert(modelRunUsageDetails).values({
249	      modelRunId: modelRun.id,
250	      contextPackId: contextPack.id,
251	      routeId: actualRouteId,
252	      inputTokens: result.usage.inputTokens ?? null,
253	      outputTokens: result.usage.outputTokens ?? null,
254	      cachedInputTokens: result.usage.cachedInputTokens ?? null,
255	      cacheWriteTokens: result.usage.cacheWriteTokens ?? null,
256	      reasoningTokens: result.usage.reasoningTokens ?? null,
257	      providerRequestId: result.usage.providerRequestId ?? null,
258	      rawUsageJson: (result.usage.rawUsageJson ?? null) as Record<string, unknown> | null,
259	      fellBackFromRouteId: result.fellBackFromRouteId ?? null,
260	      fallbackReason: result.fallbackReason ?? null,
261	    });
262	
263	    await db
264	      .update(oracleContextPacks)
265	      .set({ modelRunId: modelRun.id })
266	      .where(eq(oracleContextPacks.id, contextPack.id));
267	
268	    if (!result.validation.ok) {
269	      return { object: null, modelRunId, error: result.validation.error?.message ?? 'schema validation failed' };
270	    }
271	    return { object: result.object as ContradictionCheck, modelRunId, error: null };
272	  } catch (err) {
273	    const latencyMs = Date.now() - callStartedAt;
274	    const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
275	    // Best-effort failure row so admin dashboards see the attempt.
276	    try {
277	      const [modelRun] = await db
278	        .insert(modelRuns)
279	        .values({
280	          taskType: 'contradiction-check',
281	          model: route.modelId,
282	          provider: route.provider,
283	          promptVersion: CONTRADICTION_PROMPT_VERSION,
284	          latencyMs,
285	          success: false,
286	          error: message,
287	        })
288	        .returning({ id: modelRuns.id });
289	      modelRunId = modelRun?.id ?? null;
290	    } catch {
291	      /* observability write itself failed — swallow to surface the real error */
292	    }
293	    return { object: null, modelRunId, error: message };
294	  }
295	}
296	
297	function hashString(s: string): string {
298	  return createHash('sha256').update(s).digest('hex');
299	}
300	
301	// ─── Check a single claim for contradictions with existing approved claims.
302	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
303	async function checkClaimForContradictions(
304	  claimId: string,
305	  _triggerRunId: string,
306	): Promise<{ contradictionsFound: number }> {
307	  const db = getDirectDb();
308	  const client = buildOracleClient();
309	
310	  // Load the claim.
311	  const [claim] = await db
312	    .select()
313	    .from(claims)
314	    .where(and(eq(claims.id, claimId), eq(claims.status, 'approved')))
315	    .limit(1);
316	
317	  if (!claim) {
318	    console.log(`[contradiction-watcher] claim ${claimId} not found or not approved — skipping`);
319	    return { contradictionsFound: 0 };
320	  }
321	
322	  // Read settings + resolve routes.
323	  const [
324	    enableLiveSetting,
325	    cooldownSetting,
326	    rateCapSetting,
327	    extractionRoute,
328	    interviewRoute,
329	  ] = await Promise.all([
330	    db
331	      .select({ value: settings.value })
332	      .from(settings)
333	      .where(eq(settings.key, 'enable_live_contradiction_interjections'))
334	      .limit(1),
335	    db
336	      .select({ value: settings.value })
337	      .from(settings)
338	      .where(eq(settings.key, 'oracle_cooldown_minutes'))
339	      .limit(1),
340	    db
341	      .select({ value: settings.value })
342	      .from(settings)
343	      .where(eq(settings.key, 'max_oracle_interjections_per_hour'))
344	      .limit(1),
345	    resolveContradictionRoute(db),
346	    resolveInterviewRoute(db),
347	  ]);
348	
349	  const enableLiveInterjection = enableLiveSetting[0]?.value === true;
350	  const oracleCooldownMinutes =
351	    typeof cooldownSetting[0]?.value === 'number'
352	      ? (cooldownSetting[0]!.value as number)
353	      : DEFAULT_ORACLE_COOLDOWN_MINUTES;
354	  const maxOracleInterjectionsPerHour =
355	    typeof rateCapSetting[0]?.value === 'number'
356	      ? (rateCapSetting[0]!.value as number)
357	      : DEFAULT_MAX_ORACLE_INTERJECTIONS_PER_HOUR;
358	  // Use extractionRoute for adjudication; interviewRoute for live drafting.
359	  const route = extractionRoute;
360	
361	  // Ensure the claim has an embedding; compute one if absent.
362	  let claimEmbedding = claim.embedding;
363	  if (!claimEmbedding) {
364	    try {
365	      const { vector } = await embedText(claim.summary);
366	      claimEmbedding = vector;
367	      await db.update(claims).set({ embedding: vector }).where(eq(claims.id, claimId));
368	    } catch (embedErr) {
369	      console.warn('[contradiction-watcher] embedding failed — skipping ANN search', embedErr);
370	      return { contradictionsFound: 0 };
371	    }
372	  }
373	
374	  // ANN search via searchWithRetrievalPlan — enforces the RetrievalPlan
375	  // metadata pre-filter contract (no silent global search).
376	  //
377	  // Domain hints come from the target claim's claim_top_domains rows so that
378	  // vendor-manual noise and unrelated licensor claims are suppressed.
379	  // If the claim has no taxonomy tags yet (pre-backfill), we fall back to
380	  // buildGlobalRetrievalPlan (searchScope='global_explicit') and log a warning.
381	  //
382	  // Pass the already-computed claimEmbedding as precomputedVector so
383	  // searchWithRetrievalPlan skips a second embedText() call for the same summary.
384	  const domainRows = await db
385	    .select({ topDomainId: claimTopDomains.topDomainId })
386	    .from(claimTopDomains)
387	    .where(eq(claimTopDomains.claimId, claimId));
388	  const domainHints = domainRows.map((r) => r.topDomainId);
389	
390	  const annPlan =
391	    domainHints.length > 0
392	      ? buildDomainScopedPlan(claim.summary, domainHints, {
393	          topK: TOP_K,
394	          precomputedVector: claimEmbedding as number[],
395	        })
396	      : (() => {
397	          console.warn('[contradiction-watcher] claim has no domain tags — falling back to global ANN', {
398	            claimId,
399	            hint: 'Run the taxonomy re-evaluation worker to assign claim_top_domains.',
400	          });
401	          return buildGlobalRetrievalPlan(claim.summary, {
402	            topK: TOP_K,
403	            precomputedVector: claimEmbedding as number[],
404	          });
405	        })();
406	
407	  const annResults = await searchWithRetrievalPlan(db, annPlan);
408	  // Exclude the target claim itself (searchWithRetrievalPlan doesn't know about it).
409	  const candidates = annResults.filter(
410	    (r) => r.id !== claimId && r.distance < SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD,
411	  );
412	  if (candidates.length === 0) return { contradictionsFound: 0 };
413	
414	  let contradictionsFound = 0;
415	
416	  for (const candidate of candidates) {
417	    // Skip if this pair already has a contradictions row.
418	    const existing = await db
419	      .select({ id: contradictions.id })
420	      .from(contradictions)
421	      .where(
422	        and(
423	          inArray(contradictions.claimAId, [claimId, candidate.id]),
424	          inArray(contradictions.claimBId, [claimId, candidate.id]),
425	        ),
426	      )
427	      .limit(1);
428	    if (existing.length > 0) continue;
429	
430	    const { object, modelRunId, error } = await adjudicateOnePair(
431	      db,
432	      client,
433	      route,
434	      claim.summary,
435	      candidate.summary,
436	    );
437	    if (error || !object) {
438	      if (error) console.error('[contradiction-watcher] adjudication failed:', error);
439	      continue;
440	    }
441	    if (!object.isContradiction) continue;
442	
443	    contradictionsFound++;
444	
445	    // Resolve channel context — pick the channel containing the most recent
446	    // claim_evidence row for either claim. If neither claim has any
447	    // claim_evidence with a source_message_id (document-only evidence),
448	    // the contradiction stays as a contradictions row but cannot be live-
449	    // posted — there's nowhere to post.
450	    const channelCtx = await findChannelForClaimPair(db, claimId, candidate.id);
451	
452	    // Compute the inputs to decideContradictionInterjection.
453	    let cooldownMin: number | null = null;
454	    let interjectionsInLastHour = 0;
455	    if (channelCtx) {
456	      const lastInt = await db
457	        .select({ createdAt: oracleInterventions.createdAt })
458	        .from(oracleInterventions)
459	        .where(eq(oracleInterventions.channelId, channelCtx.channelId))
460	        .orderBy(desc(oracleInterventions.createdAt))
461	        .limit(1);
462	      cooldownMin =
463	        lastInt.length > 0 && lastInt[0]
464	          ? Math.floor((Date.now() - lastInt[0].createdAt.getTime()) / 60_000)
465	          : null;
466	      const oneHourAgo = new Date(Date.now() - 60 * 60 * 1000);
467	      const countRows = await db
468	        .select({ c: sql<number>`count(*)::int` })
469	        .from(oracleInterventions)
470	        .where(
471	          and(
472	            eq(oracleInterventions.channelId, channelCtx.channelId),
473	            gte(oracleInterventions.createdAt, oneHourAgo),
474	          ),
475	        );
476	      interjectionsInLastHour = countRows[0]?.c ?? 0;
477	    }
478	
479	    const interjectionDecisionResult = decideContradictionInterjection({
480	      detectionConfidence: CONTRADICTION_LIVE_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD,
481	      severity: object.severity,
482	      enableLiveContradictionInterjections: enableLiveInterjection,
483	      minutesSinceLastOracleInterjection: cooldownMin,
484	      oracleCooldownMinutes,
485	      interjectionsInLastHour,
486	      maxOracleInterjectionsPerHour,
487	      suggestedQuestion: object.suggestedQuestion ?? null,
488	    });
489	
490	    // If we have no channel to post in, force the decision to queue.
491	    const willLive =
492	      interjectionDecisionResult.decision === 'live' && channelCtx !== null;
493	    const decisionLabel = willLive ? 'live_interjection' : 'queued_gap';
494	
495	    const [contradiction] = await db
496	      .insert(contradictions)
497	      .values({
498	        claimAId: claimId,
499	        claimBId: candidate.id,
500	        description: object.explanation,
501	        severity: object.severity,
502	        status: 'possible',
503	        detectionConfidence: CONTRADICTION_LIVE_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD,
504	        retrievedClaimIds: candidates.map((c) => c.id),
505	        interjectionDecision: decisionLabel,
506	        suggestedQuestion: object.suggestedQuestion ?? null,
507	        createdByModelRunId: modelRunId,
508	      })
509	      .returning({ id: contradictions.id });
510	
511	    // Live branch: draft a one-liner via the interview route and post.
512	    let interjectionMessageId: string | null = null;
513	    if (willLive && channelCtx && object.suggestedQuestion) {
514	      try {
515	        const drafted = await draftContradictionInterjection(
516	          db,
517	          client,
518	          interviewRoute,
519	          {
520	            suggestedQuestion: object.suggestedQuestion,
521	            claimASummary: claim.summary,
522	            claimBSummary: candidate.summary,
523	            explanation: object.explanation,
524	          },
525	        );
526	        const [postedMessage] = await db
527	          .insert(messages)
528	          .values({
529	            channelId: channelCtx.channelId,
530	            employeeId: null,
531	            role: 'assistant',
532	            content: drafted.text,
533	            metadataJson: {
534	              source: 'contradiction-interjection',
535	              contradictionId: contradiction?.id,
536	              modelRunId: drafted.modelRunId,
537	              decisionReason: interjectionDecisionResult.reason,
538	            },
539	          })
540	          .returning({ id: messages.id });
541	        interjectionMessageId = postedMessage?.id ?? null;
542	      } catch (drafterr) {
543	        console.error('[contradiction-watcher] live drafting failed:', drafterr);
544	        // Fall through — record the intervention as not-live; the gap still gets queued below.
545	      }
546	    }
547	
548	    // If queued (decision wasn't live, or live drafting failed), create a gap.
549	    if (!interjectionMessageId && object.suggestedQuestion) {
550	      await db.insert(gaps).values({
551	        gapType: 'contradiction_gap',
552	        relatedClaimIds: [claimId, candidate.id],
553	        relatedContradictionId: contradiction?.id,
554	        questionToAsk: object.suggestedQuestion,
555	        whyItMatters: `Two approved claims appear to contradict: "${claim.summary}" vs "${candidate.summary}"`,
556	        priority: object.severity === 'high' ? 'high' : 'medium',
557	        status: 'open',
558	        createdByModelRunId: modelRunId,
559	      });
560	    }
561	
562	    // Log oracle_interventions row. channelId is the real channel if we found
563	    // one, or the all-zero placeholder if the contradiction is between
564	    // claims sourced only from documents (no chat channel).
565	    await db.insert(oracleInterventions).values({
566	      channelId: channelCtx?.channelId ?? '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000',
567	      triggerType: 'possible_contradiction',
568	      relatedContradictionId: contradiction?.id,
569	      interjectionMessageId,
570	      confidence: CONTRADICTION_LIVE_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD,
571	      impactScore: object.severity === 'high' ? 9 : object.severity === 'medium' ? 6 : 3,
572	      wasLiveInterjection: interjectionMessageId !== null,
573	      reason: interjectionDecisionResult.decision === 'live'
574	        ? interjectionDecisionResult.reason
575	        : `${interjectionDecisionResult.reason} [details: ${object.explanation}]`,
576	    });
577	  }
578	
579	  return { contradictionsFound };
580	}
581	
582	// ─── Per-claim task ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
583	export const contradictionWatcherTask = task({
584	  id: 'contradiction-watcher',
585	  maxDuration: 60,
586	  run: async (payload: z.infer<typeof ClaimCheckPayloadSchema>, { ctx }) => {
587	    ClaimCheckPayloadSchema.parse(payload);
588	    const db = getDirectDb();
589	
590	    const [jobRun] = await db
591	      .insert(jobRuns)
592	      .values({
593	        triggerRunId: ctx.run.id,
594	        jobType: 'contradiction-watcher',
595	        status: 'running',
596	        startedAt: new Date(),
597	        inputJson: { claimId: payload.claimId },
598	      })
599	      .returning({ id: jobRuns.id });
600	
601	    if (!jobRun) throw new Error('[contradiction-watcher] failed to insert job_runs row');
602	
603	    try {
604	      const result = await checkClaimForContradictions(payload.claimId, ctx.run.id);
605	      await db
606	        .update(jobRuns)
607	        .set({ status: 'complete', finishedAt: new Date(), outputJson: result })
608	        .where(eq(jobRuns.id, jobRun.id));
609	      return { ok: true, claimId: payload.claimId, ...result };
610	    } catch (err) {
611	      await db
612	        .update(jobRuns)
613	        .set({
614	          status: 'failed',
615	          finishedAt: new Date(),
616	          error: err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err),
617	        })
618	        .where(eq(jobRuns.id, jobRun.id));
619	      throw err;
620	    }
621	  },
622	});
623	
624	// ─── Sweep cron ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
625	export const contradictionWatcherSweepTask = schedules.task({
626	  id: 'contradiction-watcher-sweep',
627	  cron: '0 */4 * * *',
628	  maxDuration: 60 * 2,
629	  run: async (_payload, { ctx: _ctx }) => {
630	    const db = getDirectDb();
631	
632	    const unchecked = await db.execute<{ id: string }>(
633	      sql`
634	        SELECT c.id
635	        FROM claims c
636	        WHERE c.status = 'approved'
637	          AND c.embedding IS NOT NULL
638	          AND NOT EXISTS (
639	            SELECT 1 FROM contradictions ct
640	            WHERE ct.claim_a_id = c.id OR ct.claim_b_id = c.id
641	          )
642	        ORDER BY c.created_at DESC
643	        LIMIT 50;
644	      `,
645	    );
646	
647	    if (unchecked.length === 0) {
648	      return { ok: true, triggered: 0 };
649	    }
650	
651	    let triggered = 0;
652	    for (const row of unchecked) {
653	      try {
654	        await tasks.trigger('contradiction-watcher', { claimId: row.id });
655	        triggered++;
656	      } catch (err) {
657	        console.error(`[contradiction-watcher-sweep] failed to trigger for claim ${row.id}:`, err);
658	      }
659	    }
660	
661	    return { ok: true, triggered, total: unchecked.length };
662	  },
663	});
664	
665	// ─── R11.3 helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
666	
667	/**
668	 * Resolve the curated INTERVIEW route (Anthropic Claude Haiku 4.5 by default).
669	 * Used for human-facing contradiction interjection drafting.
670	 */
671	async function resolveInterviewRoute(
672	  db: ReturnType<typeof getDirectDb>,
673	): Promise<OracleModelRoute> {
674	  const resolved = await resolveRouteFromSettings(db, 'interview');
675	  if (resolved) return resolved;
676	  const fallback = getOracleRoute(FALLBACK_INTERVIEW_ROUTE_ID);
677	  if (!fallback) {
678	    throw new Error(
679	      `[contradiction-watcher] default_interview_route unset/unresolvable, and fallback "${FALLBACK_INTERVIEW_ROUTE_ID}" also missing.`,
680	    );
681	  }
682	  console.warn(
683	    `[contradiction-watcher] default_interview_route unset/unresolvable; using fallback "${FALLBACK_INTERVIEW_ROUTE_ID}".`,
684	  );
685	  return fallback;
686	}
687	
688	/**
689	 * Find a chat channel to post a live interjection in for a pair of contradicting
690	 * claims. Picks the channel containing the most-recent claim_evidence
691	 * source_message for either claim. Returns null if neither claim has
692	 * message-sourced evidence (e.g. both came purely from document chunks).
693	 */
694	async function findChannelForClaimPair(
695	  db: ReturnType<typeof getDirectDb>,
696	  claimAId: string,
697	  claimBId: string,
698	): Promise<{ channelId: string } | null> {
699	  const rows = await db
700	    .select({
701	      channelId: messages.channelId,
702	      createdAt: messages.createdAt,
703	    })
704	    .from(claimEvidence)
705	    .innerJoin(messages, eq(claimEvidence.sourceMessageId, messages.id))
706	    .where(inArray(claimEvidence.claimId, [claimAId, claimBId]))
707	    .orderBy(desc(messages.createdAt))
708	    .limit(1);
709	  return rows.length > 0 && rows[0] ? { channelId: rows[0].channelId } : null;
710	}
711	
712	/**
713	 * Draft the live interjection message via the interview route (Anthropic
714	 * Claude Haiku 4.5). Writes the standard observability triple (context pack,
715	 * model_runs, model_run_usage_details).
716	 */
717	async function draftContradictionInterjection(
718	  db: ReturnType<typeof getDirectDb>,
719	  client: OracleAIClient,
720	  route: OracleModelRoute,
721	  input: {
722	    suggestedQuestion: string;
723	    claimASummary: string;
724	    claimBSummary: string;
725	    explanation: string;
726	  },
727	): Promise<{ text: string; modelRunId: string | null }> {
728	  const userMessage = `Suggested question (use this as-is or tighten if needed): ${input.suggestedQuestion}
729	
730	Why it matters (context for tone — do NOT include in the output):
731	Two claims on file appear to contradict:
732	- Claim A: ${input.claimASummary}
733	- Claim B: ${input.claimBSummary}
734	Explanation: ${input.explanation}`;
735	
736	  const blocks = [
737	    makeBlock({
738	      id: 'sys',
739	      label: 'Contradiction live interjection system prompt',
740	      kind: 'stable_system',
741	      content: LIVE_INTERJECTION_SYSTEM,
742	      cacheEligible: true,
743	      reasonIncluded: 'spec 5.1 Rule 1 — live contradiction interjection drafting',
744	    }),
745	    makeBlock({
746	      id: 'input',
747	      label: 'Contradiction input',
748	      kind: 'dynamic_input',
749	      content: userMessage,
750	      cacheEligible: false,
751	      reasonIncluded: 'current contradiction pair under live drafting',
752	    }),
753	  ];
754	
755	  const callStartedAt = Date.now();
756	  let modelRunId: string | null = null;
757	  try {
758	    const result = await client.runText({
759	      taskType: 'interview_chat',
760	      routeId: route.routeId,
761	      promptVersion: LIVE_INTERJECTION_PROMPT_VERSION,
762	      blocks,
763	      providerOptions: { temperature: 0.4 },
764	    });
765	    const latencyMs = Date.now() - callStartedAt;
766	    const actualRouteId = result.routeId ?? route.routeId;
767	    const actualProvider = result.provider ?? route.provider;
768	    const actualModelId = result.modelId ?? route.modelId;
769	
770	    const [contextPack] = await db
771	      .insert(oracleContextPacks)
772	      .values({
773	        taskType: 'interview_chat',
774	        routeId: route.routeId,
775	        promptVersion: LIVE_INTERJECTION_PROMPT_VERSION,
776	        stablePrefixHash: hashString(LIVE_INTERJECTION_SYSTEM),
777	        dynamicInputHash: hashString(userMessage),
778	        blocksJson: blocks.map((b) => ({
779	          id: b.id,
780	          kind: b.kind,
781	          hash: b.hash,
782	          tokenEstimate: b.tokenEstimate,
783	        })),
784	      })
785	      .returning({ id: oracleContextPacks.id });
786	    if (!contextPack) throw new Error('failed to insert oracle_context_packs row');
787	
788	    const [modelRun] = await db
789	      .insert(modelRuns)
790	      .values({
791	        taskType: 'contradiction-live-interjection',
792	        model: actualModelId,
793	        provider: actualProvider,
794	        promptVersion: LIVE_INTERJECTION_PROMPT_VERSION,
795	        inputHash: hashString(LIVE_INTERJECTION_SYSTEM),
796	        inputTokens: result.usage.inputTokens ?? null,
797	        outputTokens: result.usage.outputTokens ?? null,
798	        latencyMs,
799	        success: true,
800	      })
801	      .returning({ id: modelRuns.id });
802	    if (!modelRun) throw new Error('failed to insert model_runs row');
803	    modelRunId = modelRun.id;
804	
805	    await db.insert(modelRunUsageDetails).values({
806	      modelRunId: modelRun.id,
807	      contextPackId: contextPack.id,
808	      routeId: actualRouteId,
809	      inputTokens: result.usage.inputTokens ?? null,
810	      outputTokens: result.usage.outputTokens ?? null,
811	      cachedInputTokens: result.usage.cachedInputTokens ?? null,
812	      cacheWriteTokens: result.usage.cacheWriteTokens ?? null,
813	      reasoningTokens: result.usage.reasoningTokens ?? null,
814	      providerRequestId: result.usage.providerRequestId ?? null,
815	      rawUsageJson: (result.usage.rawUsageJson ?? null) as Record<string, unknown> | null,
816	      fellBackFromRouteId: result.fellBackFromRouteId ?? null,
817	      fallbackReason: result.fallbackReason ?? null,
818	    });
819	
820	    await db
821	      .update(oracleContextPacks)
822	      .set({ modelRunId: modelRun.id })
823	      .where(eq(oracleContextPacks.id, contextPack.id));
824	
825	    const text = (result.text ?? '').trim();
826	    if (text.length === 0) {
827	      throw new Error('drafting returned empty text');
828	    }
829	    return { text, modelRunId };
830	  } catch (err) {
831	    const latencyMs = Date.now() - callStartedAt;
832	    const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
833	    try {
834	      const [modelRun] = await db
835	        .insert(modelRuns)
836	        .values({
837	          taskType: 'contradiction-live-interjection',
838	          model: route.modelId,
839	          provider: route.provider,
840	          promptVersion: LIVE_INTERJECTION_PROMPT_VERSION,
841	          latencyMs,
842	          success: false,
843	          error: message,
844	        })
845	        .returning({ id: modelRuns.id });
846	      modelRunId = modelRun?.id ?? null;
847	    } catch {
848	      /* swallow */
849	    }
850	    throw err;
851	  }
852	}

### Fix #1a: contradiction-watcher — lock + re-check before insert+post

The decision-commit sequence is the per-candidate block: compute cooldown/rate inputs → `decideContradictionInterjection` → insert contradiction → draft+post → insert gap → insert oracleInterventions. The model adjudication (`adjudicateOnePair`) and the live drafting (`draftContradictionInterjection`) are the multi-second LLM calls. The lock must wrap the rate re-read + the inserts.

The problem: drafting (LLM) happens between the decision and the message insert. The instruction says keep the model call outside the lock where possible, but re-check + insert must be inside the lock. The live-draft only fires when `willLive`. For contradiction-watcher, the cleanest: do adjudication outside (already is), then for the commit, draft the message first (outside lock, only if willLive), THEN open a transaction that acquires the advisory lock keyed on channelId, re-reads the rate/cooldown counts, re-evaluates whether still under cap, and inserts contradiction + gap + intervention + the posted message.

But there's a subtlety: the contradiction row + gap should be written even when not posting live (they're knowledge artifacts, not interjections). The rate-cap only governs the live interjection. The advisory lock's purpose is to prevent two workers both posting an interjection to the same channel past the cap.

The `channelCtx` can be null (document-only). When null, no posting happens — no lock needed. When present, wrap. Let me restructure carefully. Let me re-read the exact block once more around 479-577 with current line numbers.
